docs.text:
Command Reference ¶ Quick reference ¶ haven init [source] [--branch ] [--apply] [--profile

] haven list [--profile

] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] haven add [--link] [--apply] [--update] haven remove [--dry-run] haven apply [--profile

] [--module ] [--dry-run] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] [--apply-externals] [--remove-unreferenced-brews] [--interactive] [--zap] haven diff [--profile

] [--module ] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] [--stat] [--color always|never|auto] haven status [--profile

] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] haven source-path haven brew install [--cask] [--module ] haven brew uninstall [--cask] haven import --from chezmoi [--source

] [--dry-run] [--include-ignored-files] haven ai discover haven ai add [--name ] [--platforms

] [--deploy symlink|copy] haven ai fetch [] haven ai update [] haven ai remove [--yes] haven ai search [--limit ] haven ai scan [--dry-run] haven data haven unmanaged [--path

] [--depth ] haven upgrade [--check] [--force] haven telemetry [--enable] [--disable] [--note|--action|--bug|--question ""] [--list] [--list-notes] [--list-actions] [--list-bugs] [--list-questions] haven security-scan [--entropy] haven completions fish|zsh|bash haven vcs Global options ¶ Option Default Description --dir ~/.local/share/haven haven repo directory. Also read from HAVEN_DIR env var. haven init ¶ Create or clone a haven repository. Use this once, on first-time setup. For subsequent re-provisioning of an already-initialized machine, use haven apply . haven init [source] [--branch ] [--apply] [--profile

] Argument/Option Description source Optional. Git URL or gh:owner/repo[@ref] . Omit to create a blank scaffold. --branch Branch to clone. Overrides any @ref in source. --apply Apply the cloned repo immediately after cloning. Requires a source. --profile

Profile to apply. Requires --apply . haven add ¶ Start tracking a dotfile by copying it into source/ . For directories: if the directory is a git repo, interactively prompts to track it as an external clone (re-cloned on apply) or recursively copy its files. haven add [--link] [--apply] [--update] Argument/Option Description file Path to the file or directory to track. --link Track as a symlink: on apply, the destination is symlinked back to source/ . --apply Immediately install after adding. Only valid with --link . --update Re-copy the file even if already tracked. Without this flag, adding an already-tracked file is an error. haven remove ¶ Stop tracking a dotfile by removing it from source/ . The live file on disk is not touched. haven remove [--dry-run] Argument/Option Description file Destination path to stop tracking. --dry-run Print what would be removed without deleting any files. haven apply ¶ Apply tracked files and packages to this machine. By default all sections are applied. Use --files , --brews , and/or --ai to apply only specific sections. haven apply [--profile

] [--module ] [--dry-run] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] [--apply-externals] [--remove-unreferenced-brews] [--interactive] [--zap] Option Description --profile

Profile to apply. Default: last-used profile, or default . --module Scope brew and mise to this module. File operations are always global. --dry-run Print the plan without writing any files. --files Apply dotfile copies/symlinks. --brews Run brew bundle install . --ai Deploy AI skills. --apply-externals Pull (update) existing extdir_ git clones. Without this, existing clones are left as-is. --remove-unreferenced-brews After installing, uninstall leaf formula/cask not in any active Brewfile. --interactive Like --remove-unreferenced-brews but prompts before removing. --zap Like --remove-unreferenced-brews but also passes --zap to cask uninstall, removing app data. haven diff ¶ Show the diff between tracked source files/packages and live state. Exits 0 when up to date, 1 when drift is found. haven diff [--profile

] [--module ] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] [--stat] [--color always|never|auto] Option Description --profile

Profile to diff. --module Scope brew and AI diff to this module. --files Diff dotfiles. --brews Diff Homebrew packages. --ai Diff AI skills. --stat Show summary (file names + change counts) instead of full diff. --color always , never , or auto (default). haven status ¶ Show drift between tracked source files and live destinations. Drift markers: ✓ clean · M modified · ? missing · ! source missing haven status [--profile

] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] haven list ¶ List tracked files, Homebrew packages, and AI skills. haven list [--profile

] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] Option Description --profile

Scope to a specific profile. --files Show tracked files only. --brews Show Homebrew packages only. --ai Show AI skills only. haven source-path ¶ Print the absolute path to the haven repo directory and exit. haven source-path # Use in scripts: cd $(haven source-path) $EDITOR $(haven source-path)/haven.toml haven data ¶ Show all template variables available in .tmpl files on this machine. haven data haven brew install ¶ Install a Homebrew formula or cask and record it in a Brewfile. haven brew install [--cask] [--module ] Option Description --cask Install as a cask. --module Record in this module's Brewfile. Default: master brew/Brewfile . haven brew uninstall ¶ Uninstall a formula or cask and remove it from all Brewfiles. haven brew uninstall [--cask] haven import ¶ Import dotfiles from another dotfile manager (one-time migration). Currently only chezmoi is supported. haven import --from chezmoi [--source

] [--dry-run] [--include-ignored-files] Option Description --from Source format. Currently only chezmoi . --source Path to source directory. Auto-detected if not given. --dry-run Preview without writing any files. --include-ignored-files Import files matching .chezmoiignore patterns. haven ai discover ¶ Scan this machine for installed AI agent platforms and update ai/platforms.toml . haven ai add ¶ Add a skill declaration. haven ai add [--name ] [--platforms

] [--deploy symlink|copy] Option Description source gh:owner/repo[/subpath][@ref] or dir:~/path . --name Local name. Default: inferred from source. --platforms

all , cross-client , or comma-separated platform IDs. Default: all . --deploy symlink (default) or copy . haven ai fetch ¶ Download skills to cache without deploying. haven ai fetch [] haven ai update ¶ Pull latest versions + update lock SHAs. haven ai update [] haven ai remove ¶ Remove a skill. haven ai remove [--yes] haven ai search ¶ Search the skills.sh registry. haven ai search [--limit ] haven ai scan ¶ Interactively import unmanaged skills from a directory. haven ai scan [--dry-run] haven telemetry ¶ Manage local telemetry. Without flags, prints current status. haven telemetry [--enable] [--disable] [--note|--action|--bug|--question ""] [--list] [--list-notes] [--list-actions] [--list-bugs] [--list-questions] Flag Description --enable Enable telemetry in haven.toml . --disable Disable telemetry in haven.toml . --note "" Append a free-form note (ID prefix N ). --action "" Record a deliberate action (ID prefix A ). --bug "" Record a bug observed (ID prefix B ). --question "" Record a question for later investigation (ID prefix Q ). --list Print all entries. --list-notes Print only notes. --list-actions Print only actions. --list-bugs Print only bugs. --list-questions Print only questions. Annotation flags always write to ~/.haven/telemetry.jsonl regardless of whether telemetry is enabled. Each annotation gets an auto-generated sequenced ID (e.g. B000001 ). haven upgrade ¶ Upgrade haven to the latest version. haven upgrade [--check] [--force] Flag Description --check Check for update without installing. Exits 0 when up to date, 1 when update is available. --force Install even if already on latest. haven unmanaged ¶ Find files in ~ not tracked by haven. haven unmanaged [--path

] [--depth ] Option Description --path

Directory to scan. Default: ~ . --depth Maximum scan depth. Default: 3. haven security-scan ¶ Scan all tracked source files for secrets and sensitive content. haven security-scan [--entropy] Option Description --entropy Also flag high-entropy strings (≥16 chars, Shannon entropy >4.5 bits/char). Exits 0 when clean, 1 when findings are reported. haven completions ¶ Print a shell completion script to stdout. haven completions # fish, zsh, or bash haven vcs ¶ Show the active VCS backend and its configuration source. haven vcs

Apply tracked files and packages to this machine. By default all sections are applied. Use --files , --brews , and/or --ai to apply only specific sections. haven apply [--profile

] [--module ] [--dry-run] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] [--apply-externals] [--remove-unreferenced-brews] [--interactive] [--zap] Option Description --profile

Profile to apply. Default: last-used profile, or default . --module Scope brew and mise to this module. File operations are always global. --dry-run Print the plan without writing any files. --files Apply dotfile copies/symlinks. --brews Run brew bundle install . --ai Deploy AI skills. --apply-externals Pull (update) existing extdir_ git clones. Without this, existing clones are left as-is. --remove-unreferenced-brews After installing, uninstall leaf formula/cask not in any active Brewfile. --interactive Like --remove-unreferenced-brews but prompts before removing. --zap Like --remove-unreferenced-brews but also passes --zap to cask uninstall, removing app data.
docs.title: haven apply

docs.text:
Show the diff between tracked source files/packages and live state. Exits 0 when up to date, 1 when drift is found. haven diff [--profile

] [--module ] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] [--stat] [--color always|never|auto] Option Description --profile

Profile to diff. --module Scope brew and AI diff to this module. --files Diff dotfiles. --brews Diff Homebrew packages. --ai Diff AI skills. --stat Show summary (file names + change counts) instead of full diff. --color always , never , or auto (default).
docs.title: haven diff

docs.text:
Show drift between tracked source files and live destinations. Drift markers: ✓ clean · M modified · ? missing · ! source missing haven status [--profile

] [--files] [--brews] [--ai]
docs.title: haven status

docs.text:
List tracked files, Homebrew packages, and AI skills. haven list [--profile

] [--files] [--brews] [--ai] Option Description --profile

Scope to a specific profile. --files Show tracked files only. --brews Show Homebrew packages only. --ai Show AI skills only.
docs.title: haven list

docs.text: Print the absolute path to the haven repo directory and exit. haven source-path # Use in scripts: cd $(haven source-path) $EDITOR $(haven source-path)/haven.toml
docs.title: haven source-path

docs.text: Show all template variables available in .tmpl files on this machine. haven data
docs.title: haven data

docs.text: Install a Homebrew formula or cask and record it in a Brewfile. haven brew install <name> [--cask] [--module <m>] Option Description --cask Install as a cask. --module <m> Record in this module's Brewfile. Default: master brew/Brewfile .
docs.title: haven brew install

docs.text: Uninstall a formula or cask and remove it from all Brewfiles. haven brew uninstall <name> [--cask]
docs.title: haven brew uninstall

docs.text: Import dotfiles from another dotfile manager (one-time migration). Currently only chezmoi is supported. haven import --from chezmoi [--source <dir>] [--dry-run] [--include-ignored-files] Option Description --from <manager> Source format. Currently only chezmoi . --source <dir> Path to source directory. Auto-detected if not given. --dry-run Preview without writing any files. --include-ignored-files Import files matching .chezmoiignore patterns.
docs.title: haven import

docs.text: Scan this machine for installed AI agent platforms and update ai/platforms.toml .
docs.title: haven ai discover

docs.text:
Add a skill declaration. haven ai add [--name ] [--platforms

] [--deploy symlink|copy] Option Description source gh:owner/repo[/subpath][@ref] or dir:~/path . --name Local name. Default: inferred from source. --platforms

all , cross-client , or comma-separated platform IDs. Default: all . --deploy symlink (default) or copy .
docs.title: haven ai add

docs.text: Download skills to cache without deploying. haven ai fetch [<name>]
docs.title: haven ai fetch

docs.text: Pull latest versions + update lock SHAs. haven ai update [<name>]
docs.title: haven ai update

docs.text: Remove a skill. haven ai remove <name> [--yes]
docs.title: haven ai remove

docs.text: Search the skills.sh registry. haven ai search <query> [--limit <n>]
docs.title: haven ai search

docs.text: Interactively import unmanaged skills from a directory. haven ai scan <path> [--dry-run]
docs.title: haven ai scan

docs.text: Manage local telemetry. Without flags, prints current status. haven telemetry [--enable] [--disable] [--note|--action|--bug|--question "<message>"] [--list] [--list-notes] [--list-actions] [--list-bugs] [--list-questions] Flag Description --enable Enable telemetry in haven.toml . --disable Disable telemetry in haven.toml . --note "<text>" Append a free-form note (ID prefix N ). --action "<text>" Record a deliberate action (ID prefix A ). --bug "<text>" Record a bug observed (ID prefix B ). --question "<text>" Record a question for later investigation (ID prefix Q ). --list Print all entries. --list-notes Print only notes. --list-actions Print only actions. --list-bugs Print only bugs. --list-questions Print only questions. Annotation flags always write to ~/.haven/telemetry.jsonl regardless of whether telemetry is enabled. Each annotation gets an auto-generated sequenced ID (e.g. B000001 ).
docs.title: haven telemetry

docs.text: Upgrade haven to the latest version. haven upgrade [--check] [--force] Flag Description --check Check for update without installing. Exits 0 when up to date, 1 when update is available. --force Install even if already on latest.
docs.title: haven upgrade

docs.text:
Find files in ~ not tracked by haven. haven unmanaged [--path

] [--depth ] Option Description --path

Directory to scan. Default: ~ . --depth Maximum scan depth. Default: 3.
docs.title: haven unmanaged

docs.text: Scan all tracked source files for secrets and sensitive content. haven security-scan [--entropy] Option Description --entropy Also flag high-entropy strings (≥16 chars, Shannon entropy >4.5 bits/char). Exits 0 when clean, 1 when findings are reported.
docs.title: haven security-scan

docs.text: Print a shell completion script to stdout. haven completions <shell> # fish, zsh, or bash
docs.title: haven completions

docs.text: Show the active VCS backend and its configuration source. haven vcs
docs.title: haven vcs

docs.text: Configuration Reference ¶ haven.toml ¶ The top-level configuration file at the repo root. All sections are optional. # haven.toml [profile.default] modules = ["shell", "git", "packages"] [profile.work] extends = "default" modules = ["work", "secrets"] [data] work_email = "alice@corp.example" kanata_path = "/usr/local/bin/kanata" [security] allow = [ "~/.config/gh/hosts.yml", ] [vcs] backend = "git" # or "jj" [telemetry] enabled = false [profile.<name>] ¶ Declares a profile — a named set of modules to apply. Field Type Description modules string array Module names to activate for this profile. extends string Parent profile name. Parent's modules are applied first. [data] ¶ Custom variables available in all .tmpl files as {{ data.<key> }} . Only flat string values are supported. Arrays and nested tables are not. [security] ¶ Field Type Description allow string array Glob patterns for files to skip in security scanning. Same syntax as config/ignore . [vcs] ¶ Field Type Description backend "git" or "jj" VCS backend for clone and init operations. [telemetry] ¶ Field Type Description enabled bool Enable local telemetry logging to ~/.haven/telemetry.jsonl . Default: false . modules/<name>.toml ¶ Per-module configuration. All sections are optional. # modules/shell.toml [homebrew] brewfile = "brew/Brewfile.shell" [mise] config = "source/mise.toml" requires_op = false Field Type Description [homebrew] brewfile string Path to Brewfile, relative to repo root. [mise] config string Path to mise config file, relative to repo root. requires_op bool If true , skip brew/mise if op CLI is unavailable or not signed in. Default: false . ai/platforms.toml ¶ Declares which AI agent platforms are active on this machine. active = ["claude-code"] [platform.claude-code] skills_dir = "~/.claude/skills" Field Description active Array of platform IDs to activate. [platform.<id>] skills_dir Directory where skills for this platform are deployed. Run haven ai discover to auto-generate this file by scanning installed platforms. ai/skills/<name>/skill.toml ¶ Declares a single AI skill. source = "gh:anthropics/skills/pdf-processing@v1.0" platforms = "all" deploy = "symlink" Field Type Description source string Skill source: gh:owner/repo[/subpath][@ref] or dir:~/path . platforms string or array "all" , "cross-client" , or array of platform IDs. deploy "symlink" or "copy" Deploy method. Default: "symlink" . config/ignore ¶ gitignore-style patterns for files to exclude from apply , status , and diff . # config/ignore {% if os == "macos" %} .DS_Store {% endif %} .ssh/id_* .config/*/history .local/share/some-app/** !.local/share/some-app/keep-this This file is a Tera template — evaluated at runtime on every command that loads it. If the template fails to render, haven warns and falls back to ignoring nothing. Pattern rules ¶ Syntax Matches # at start Comment (ignored) * Any non- / characters ** Any characters including / ? Any single non- / character ! prefix Negate — un-ignores a previously matched path Pattern with no / Basename only Pattern with / Full path from home root haven.lock ¶ Auto-generated. Records SHA256 hashes of all fetched external sources. # haven.lock — auto-generated, do not edit by hand [skill."gh:anthropics/skills/pdf-processing@v1.0"] sha = "abc123def456..." fetched_at = "2026-03-21T10:00:00Z" Commit this file alongside your config. Run haven ai update to intentionally upgrade a skill and refresh its SHA.
docs.title: Configuration

docs.text: Environment Variables ¶ Environment variables for configuring haven's behavior without editing config files. Variable Default Description HAVEN_DIR ~/.local/share/haven haven repo root directory. Equivalent to --dir flag. HAVEN_CLAUDE_DIR ~/.claude Claude Code directory (skills, CLAUDE.md). HAVEN_TELEMETRY unset Set to 1 to enable telemetry, 0 to force-disable (overrides haven.toml ). HAVEN_VCS unset Set to git or jj to override the VCS backend. Priority order ¶ When the same setting can be configured in multiple places, haven uses this priority (first match wins): VCS backend: --vcs CLI flag HAVEN_VCS environment variable vcs.backend in haven.toml Interactive detection (if jj is on PATH and nothing is configured, haven prompts) Default: git Repo directory: --dir CLI flag HAVEN_DIR environment variable Default: ~/.local/share/haven Telemetry: HAVEN_TELEMETRY=0 (force-disable) HAVEN_TELEMETRY=1 (force-enable) [telemetry] enabled in haven.toml Default: disabled Examples ¶ # Use a custom repo location for this session HAVEN_DIR=~/work-env haven apply --profile work # Use jj for this command only HAVEN_VCS=jj haven init gh:alice/dotfiles # Enable telemetry for one command HAVEN_TELEMETRY=1 haven apply
docs.title: Environment Variables

Equivalent to --dir flag. HAVEN_CLAUDE_DIR ~/.claude Claude Code directory (skills, CLAUDE.md). HAVEN_TELEMETRY unset Set to 1 to enable telemetry, 0 to force-disable (overrides haven.toml ). HAVEN_VCS unset Set to git or jj to override the VCS backend.
docs.title: Environment Variables

docs.title: Priority order

docs.title: Examples

docs.text: Magic-Name Encoding ¶ haven encodes all file metadata directly in source filenames. There's no separate registry — the source/ directory tree is the complete manifest. This is the same encoding chezmoi uses, so migrating is straightforward. Prefixes ¶ Prefixes appear at the beginning of any path component (file or directory name) and can be stacked in any order. Prefix Effect on file Effect on directory dot_ Replace with . Replace with . private_ chmod 0600 chmod 0700 executable_ chmod 0755 — symlink_ Create a symlink pointing back into source/ — extdir_ Marker file — clone a remote git repo into this directory — create_only Only write if destination does not already exist — exact_ On apply, remove files in destination dir not present in source — Suffixes ¶ Suffix Effect .tmpl Render through the Tera template engine before writing. Strip suffix from destination filename. Stacking prefixes ¶ Prefixes can be combined in any order. private_executable_ and executable_private_ are equivalent. private_executable_dot_local/bin/s → ~/.local/bin/s (chmod 0700) Examples ¶ source/ path Destination Permissions dot_zshrc ~/.zshrc unchanged dot_config/git/config ~/.config/git/config unchanged private_dot_ssh/id_rsa ~/.ssh/id_rsa 0600 private_dot_ssh/ (dir) ~/.ssh/ 0700 executable_dot_local/bin/foo ~/.local/bin/foo 0755 private_executable_dot_local/bin/s ~/.local/bin/s 0700 symlink_vscode_settings.json ~/vscode_settings.json symlink dot_gitconfig.tmpl ~/.gitconfig unchanged (rendered) dot_config/extdir_nvim ~/.config/nvim git clone How haven add encodes files ¶ When you run haven add ~/.ssh/config , haven: Inspects the file's permissions Inspects the parent directory's permissions Builds the encoded path: private_dot_ssh/config Copies the file to source/private_dot_ssh/config The encoding is deterministic — you can predict and construct it manually. Editing source files ¶ You can edit source files directly in the repo: # Open the source file for ~/.zshrc $EDITOR $(haven source-path)/source/dot_zshrc # After editing, apply to this machine haven apply Or see exactly where a destination maps to: # Source path for ~/.gitconfig ls $(haven source-path)/source/dot_gitconfig*
docs.title: Magic-Name Encoding

docs.text: Repo Layout ¶ The default haven repo lives at ~/.local/share/haven . Everything here is committed to git — except ~/.haven/ (state, backups, skill cache), which is added to .gitignore by haven init . ~/.local/share/haven/ │ ├── haven.toml ← profiles, data variables, security allow-list, VCS, telemetry ├── haven.lock ← SHA-pinned sources for all fetched external content │ ├── source/ ← dotfiles with magic-name encoded filenames │ ├── dot_zshrc → ~/.zshrc │ ├── dot_gitconfig.tmpl → ~/.gitconfig (rendered through Tera) │ ├── private_dot_ssh/ │ │ └── config → ~/.ssh/config (chmod 0600) │ └── dot_config/ │ ├── git/config → ~/.config/git/config │ └── extdir_nvim → ~/.config/nvim (git clone on apply) │ ├── ai/ ← AI skill declarations │ ├── platforms.toml ← active platforms (claude-code, codex, etc.) │ └── skills/ │ ├── pdf-processing/ │ │ ├── skill.toml ← source, platforms, deploy method │ │ ├── all.md ← snippet → every active platform's config │ │ └── claude-code.md ← snippet → Claude Code's CLAUDE.md only │ └── my-commands/ │ ├── skill.toml │ └── all.md │ ├── brew/ ← Homebrew Brewfiles │ ├── Brewfile ← master (used with no --module flag) │ ├── Brewfile.shell ← module-specific │ └── Brewfile.work │ ├── modules/ ← per-module package configuration │ ├── shell.toml ← [homebrew] + [mise] for the "shell" module │ ├── git.toml │ └── work.toml │ └── config/ └── ignore ← gitignore-style patterns (Tera template) State directory ( ~/.haven/ ) ¶ Not committed. Local to each machine. ~/.haven/ ├── state.json ← last-applied profile and file state ├── backups/ ← copies of files overwritten by apply └── skills/ ← cached skill content fetched from GitHub What to commit ¶ Everything in the repo root: haven.toml , haven.lock , source/ , ai/ , brew/ , modules/ , config/ . Do not commit ~/.haven/ — haven adds it to .gitignore automatically.
docs.title: Repo Layout

docs.text: Template Variables ¶ All variables available in .tmpl files. Run haven data to see the actual values on the current machine. Built-in variables ¶ Variable Example value Description {{ os }} "macos" , "linux" Operating system {{ hostname }} "my-laptop" Machine hostname {{ username }} "alice" Current user ( $USER ) {{ home_dir }} "/Users/alice" Home directory path {{ source_dir }} "/Users/alice/.local/share/haven" haven repo root path {{ profile }} "default" , "work" Active profile name {{ arch }} "aarch64" , "x86_64" CPU architecture Functions ¶ Call Description {{ get_env(name="VAR") }} Value of environment variable VAR . Errors if unset. {{ get_env(name="VAR", default="x") }} Value of VAR , or "x" if unset. {{ op(path="vault/item/field") }} Read a secret from 1Password at apply time. {{ op(path="op://vault/item/field") }} Same with explicit op:// prefix. Custom data variables ¶ Defined in [data] in haven.toml : [data] work_email = "alice@corp.example" kanata_path = "/usr/local/bin/kanata" Accessed in templates as {{ data.<key> }} : {{ data.work_email }} {{ data.kanata_path }} OS values ¶ OS {{ os }} value macOS "macos" Linux "linux" Note: haven uses "macos" , not "darwin" . This differs from chezmoi. Arch values ¶ Architecture {{ arch }} value Apple Silicon "aarch64" Intel Mac / x86_64 Linux "x86_64" 32-bit ARM "armv7" 32-bit x86 "i686" Example: checking all variables ¶ haven data Output: os = macos hostname = my-laptop username = alice home_dir = /Users/alice source_dir = /Users/alice/.local/share/haven arch = aarch64 profile = default data.work_email = alice@corp.example data.kanata_path = /usr/local/bin/kanata Template syntax quick reference ¶ Construct Syntax Output variable {{ variable }} If {% if condition %}...{% endif %} If/else {% if condition %}...{% else %}...{% endif %} Elif {% if a %}...{% elif b %}...{% else %}...{% endif %} For loop {% for item in list %}...{% endfor %} Comment {# ignored #} Logical AND {% if a and b %} Logical OR {% if a or b %} Logical NOT {% if not a %} String equals {% if os == "macos" %} String not equal {% if os != "linux" %} Filter {{ value \| upper }} Default filter {{ value \| default(value="fallback") }} Full Tera reference: keats.github.io/tera/docs
docs.title: Template Variables

docs.text: Construct Syntax Output variable {{ variable }} If {% if condition %}...{% endif %} If/else {% if condition %}...{% else %}...{% endif %} Elif {% if a %}...{% elif b %}...{% else %}...{% endif %} For loop {% for item in list %}...{% endfor %} Comment {# ignored #} Logical AND {% if a and b %} Logical OR {% if a or b %} Logical NOT {% if not a %} String equals {% if os == "macos" %} String not equal {% if os != "linux" %} Filter {{ value \| upper }} Default filter {{ value \| default(value="fallback") }} Full Tera reference: keats.github.io/tera/docs

docs.text: haven v0.1.0 Release Notes ¶ Release date: 2026-03-20 (initial release) Overview ¶ haven is an AI-first developer environment manager. It tracks dotfiles, Homebrew packages, language runtimes, and Claude Code skills in a single git repository, and reproduces your complete development environment on any machine from a single command. haven init gh:you/my-env --apply # new machine: full environment in minutes haven apply # sync changes to this machine haven status # see what's drifted What ships in v0.1.0 ¶ Core dotfile management ¶ haven init — create or clone a haven repo. haven init # blank scaffold haven init gh:you/my-env # clone from GitHub haven init gh:you/my-env --apply # clone and apply immediately haven init https://github.com/... # any git URL haven add — start tracking a file or directory. haven add ~/.zshrc haven add ~/.config/git/config haven add ~/.config/nvim # prompts: extdir or recursive haven add ~/.zshrc --link --apply # symlink, apply immediately Sensitive filename detection ( .env , id_rsa , .pem , etc.) prompts before saving. Directory add detects git remotes and offers to track as an external clone ( extdir_ ) or recursively copy files. haven apply — deploy to this machine. haven apply # all sections haven apply --dry-run # preview without writing haven apply --files # dotfiles only haven apply --brews # Homebrew only haven apply --ai # AI skills only haven apply --run-scripts # also execute source/scripts/ Backs up existing files before overwriting. Private files are chmod 0600, executables are chmod 0755, symlinks are linked to their source entry. haven diff — show what's drifted. haven diff # full diff haven diff --stat # summary only haven diff --files # dotfiles only haven diff --color always Exits 1 when drift is found (CI-friendly). haven status — concise drift view. ✓ ~/.zshrc M ~/.gitconfig (modified) ? ~/.config/nvim (missing) Magic-name file encoding ¶ haven encodes file metadata in the source filename itself — no separate registry. Compatible with chezmoi's encoding. source/dot_zshrc → ~/.zshrc source/private_dot_ssh/id_rsa → ~/.ssh/id_rsa (chmod 0600) source/executable_dot_local/bin/foo → ~/.local/bin/foo (chmod 0755) source/symlink_vscode_settings.json → ~/vscode_settings.json (symlink) source/dot_gitconfig.tmpl → ~/.gitconfig (Tera template) source/extdir_dot_config_nvim → ~/.config/nvim (git clone) Tera templates ¶ .tmpl files are rendered through the Tera engine before writing. Available variables: Variable Example hostname mbp.local username alice os macos or linux arch aarch64 or x86_64 source_dir /Users/alice/.local/share/haven profile default env("VAR") value of $VAR op(path="op://...") 1Password secret (see below) 1Password integration ¶ {{ op(path="op://Personal/GitHub/token") }} Secrets are resolved at apply time via the op CLI. The source file stores the reference, not the value — safe to commit. Homebrew management ¶ haven brew install ripgrep # brew install + add to Brewfile haven brew install iterm2 --cask # cask support haven brew uninstall ripgrep # brew uninstall + remove from all Brewfiles haven apply --brews runs brew bundle install for all Brewfiles in the active profile. --remove-unreferenced-brews / --interactive prune unreferenced leaf packages. AI skills management ¶ haven ai add gh:anthropics/skills/pdf-processing haven ai add gh:owner/repo --platforms claude-code,codex haven ai fetch # download to cache without deploying haven ai update # re-fetch latest + update lock SHAs haven ai remove my-skill haven ai discover # detect installed AI platforms Skills are declared in ai/skills/<name>/skill.toml and deployed to platform skill directories ( ~/.claude/skills/ ) on haven apply --ai . Every gh: source is pinned by SHA in haven.lock . Profiles and modules ¶ # haven.toml [profile.default] modules = ["shell", "git"] [profile.work] extends = "default" modules = ["secrets"] Profiles control which modules (and therefore which Brewfiles and mise configs) are active on a given machine. The last-used profile is saved in state and reused automatically. chezmoi import ¶ haven import --from chezmoi haven import --from chezmoi --dry-run haven import --from chezmoi --include-ignored-files Full import pipeline from chezmoi source directories: dot_ / private_ / executable_ / symlink_ prefixes converted Go template syntax converted to Tera where possible .chezmoiexternal.toml git entries imported as extdir_ markers .chezmoiignore written as config/ignore run_once_ scripts scanned for brew bundle and mise install patterns; module TOMLs auto-generated where possible All scripts copied to source/scripts/ for haven apply --run-scripts Cross-platform binaries and installer ¶ curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnstegeman/haven/main/install.sh | sh SHA256-verified installer. Supports macOS (x86_64, arm64) and Linux (x86_64, aarch64, i686, armv7 musl). Installs to /usr/local/bin or ~/.local/bin with automatic PATH detection.

docs.text: haven v0.2.0 Release Notes ¶ Release date: 2026-03-21 Highlights ¶ haven ai add-local — import your own skills ¶ haven ai add-local ~/.claude/skills/my-skill haven ai add-local ~/dev/my-skill --name myskill --platforms claude-code Moves a locally developed skill directory into the haven repo under ai/skills/<name>/files/ , writes a skill.toml with source = "repo:" , creates a blank snippet stub, and removes the original. Run haven apply --ai afterward to deploy the symlink. This is the bridge between developing a skill locally and managing it with haven. The repo: source type means haven reads the skill directly from your repo — no download, no cache, no lock entry. extfile_ — track single-file and archive remote downloads ¶ # source/extfile_dot_local_bin_delta type = "file" url = "https://github.com/dandavison/delta/releases/download/0.18.2/delta-0.18.2-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz" sha256 = "abc123..." # optional but recommended Two types: - type = "file" — download the URL directly to the destination - type = "archive" — download a tarball and extract a specific subpath from it haven apply downloads on the first run, verifies SHA-256 if provided. haven diff shows ? when the destination is absent. Managed config section injection ¶ haven apply --ai now injects skill snippets into platform config files (e.g. ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md ) between comment markers: <!-- haven managed start --> ...snippet content... <!-- haven managed end --> The markers are added once (with a prompt if the file already exists and has no markers). All subsequent applies update the content in-place. Works for both all.md (every platform) and <platform-id>.md (platform-specific). haven ai search — discover skills from the registry ¶ haven ai search jujutsu haven ai search "pdf processing" --limit 5 Searches the skills.sh registry. Results show the skill source in gh:owner/repo/skill format and install count. Copy the source and pass it to haven ai add to start tracking. haven ai scan — import existing unmanaged skills ¶ haven ai scan ~/.claude/skills haven ai scan ~/.claude/skills --dry-run Walks the given directory for skill subdirectories. For each unmanaged skill: 1. Tries to detect the GitHub source via git remote 2. Falls back to skills.sh fuzzy search on the skill directory name 3. Prompts you to confirm, edit the source, or skip Skills already in ai/skills.toml are silently skipped. haven diff improvements ¶ AI skill SHA drift — shows ~ name (installed: abc12345, pinned: def67890) when a deployed skill is behind its lock entry. extdir_ ref drift — shows M path (extdir: at abc12345, expected v3.0.0) when a cloned external repo is behind the pinned ref. Supply chain protection ¶ haven.lock SHA verification is now enforced on every cache miss. If the freshly-fetched content SHA doesn't match the lock entry, haven ai fetch and haven apply --ai hard-fail with a message pointing to haven ai update . This prevents silent substitution attacks on gh: sources. Performance and reliability ¶ Parallel skill fetches — multiple gh: cache misses are fetched concurrently using std::thread::scope . No new dependencies. Apply file lock — PID-based lock at ~/.haven/apply.lock prevents concurrent apply runs from corrupting state. Stale locks are auto-cleaned. haven source-path and XDG default ¶ cd $(haven source-path) New source-path command prints the resolved repo path. New installs default to ~/.local/share/haven (XDG standard). Existing ~/haven repos are auto-detected without any migration step. Other changes ¶ haven add --update — re-copy an already-tracked file without an error home_dir template variable in .tmpl files create_ prefix support — files are written only if the destination doesn't exist exact_ directory prefix — untracked destination files are removed on apply Script execution — haven apply --run-scripts runs source/scripts/ ; run_once_ tracked in state Opt-in local telemetry to ~/.haven/telemetry.jsonl modify_ scripts in chezmoi import now emit a clear skip message Upgrade notes ¶ No breaking changes from v0.1.0. The XDG default path ( ~/.local/share/haven ) only applies to new installs. If you have an existing repo at ~/haven , haven will continue to use it automatically.

docs.text: haven v0.3.0 Release Notes ¶ Release date: 2026-03-23 Highlights ¶ Security fix — please upgrade from v0.2.0 ¶ A HIGH-severity vulnerability was found and patched in this release. If you use gh: skill sources, upgrade before running haven apply . A malicious tarball served by a compromised gh: skill could plant a symlink entry pointing outside the skill cache directory. A subsequent extraction step would follow the symlink and write files to an attacker-controlled path — on a developer machine, this means shell startup files can be overwritten (RCE via next shell open). The fix unconditionally skips symlink and hard-link tar entries before extraction. Two regression tests were added. See docs/designs/ for the full audit record. haven security-scan — audit your tracked files for secrets ¶ haven security-scan # scan all tracked files haven security-scan --entropy # also flag high-entropy strings Checks every file in source/ for: Check What's flagged Filename .env , id_rsa , credentials , .pem , .key , .p12 , etc. Path ~/.aws/credentials , ~/.kube/** , ~/.ssh/** , ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml , ~/.docker/config.json , etc. Content GitHub tokens ( ghp_ / ghs_ / github_pat_ ), AWS access keys ( AKIA… ), PEM private keys, OpenAI keys ( sk-… ), Anthropic keys ( sk-ant-… ), generic password = / secret = assignments High-entropy (opt-in) Random-looking tokens ≥16 chars, Shannon entropy >4.5 bits/char Exits 0 when clean, 1 when findings are reported. CI-friendly. False positives: suppress with [security] allow in haven.toml : [security] allow = [ "~/.config/gh/hosts.yml", "~/.config/gcloud/**", ] Integrated into haven add : content is scanned automatically when you add a file. If secrets are found, you are prompted before the file is saved to source/ . Declining removes it immediately. haven upgrade — self-update in one command ¶ haven upgrade # upgrade to the latest version haven upgrade --check # check without installing (exits 1 if update available) haven upgrade --force # reinstall the current version haven now upgrades itself. It fetches the latest release from GitHub, verifies the SHA256 checksum, and atomically replaces the running binary via rename. No need to re-run the install script. The --check flag is CI-friendly — it exits 0 when up to date and 1 when an update is available, making it easy to add upgrade checks to setup scripts. [data] custom template variables ¶ Define your own variables in haven.toml and use them in any .tmpl file: [data] host = "my-laptop" kanata_path = "/usr/local/bin/kanata" # In any .tmpl file: export HOST={{ data.host }} {{ if data.host }}# host-specific config{% endif %} Variables are accessible as {{ data.<key> }} . Nested objects and non-string values are not supported — only flat string keys. haven data prints all variables currently in scope: haven data os = macos hostname = my-laptop username = alice home_dir = /Users/alice source_dir = /Users/alice/.local/share/haven data.host = my-laptop data.kanata_path = /usr/local/bin/kanata Chezmoi import: haven import --from chezmoi automatically migrates .chezmoidata.yaml and .chezmoidata.toml into [data] entries in haven.toml . The template converter also updates .tmpl files — chezmoi's {{ .host }} becomes {{ data.host }} and {{ if (index . "host") }} becomes {% if data.host %} . haven unmanaged — discover dotfiles you haven't added yet ¶ haven unmanaged # scan ~ for untracked dotfiles haven unmanaged --path ~/.config # scan a specific directory haven unmanaged --depth 5 # recurse deeper (default: 3) Walks your home directory and reports every file that isn't tracked by haven. At the home root, only dotfiles and dotdirs (names starting with . ) are examined — Documents/ , Downloads/ , Projects/ , etc. are always skipped. High-noise directories are skipped at every depth: Category Directories skipped VCS internals .git , .jj , .hg , .svn Build / caches node_modules , target , .cache , .cargo , .npm , .rustup , .pyenv , .rbenv , .nvm , .volta , .asdf , .mise , .gradle , .m2 macOS system Library , .Trash , .Spotlight-V100 , .DocumentRevisions-V100 , .fseventsd , .TemporaryItems App-managed .android , .minikube , .kube , .docker , .vscode-server , .cocoapods , .bundle haven state .haven Pipe the output to haven add to bulk-import discovered files: haven unmanaged | xargs -I{} haven add {} haven list — see what you're tracking ¶ haven list ~/.zshrc ~/.gitconfig (template) ~/.ssh/config (private) ~/.local/bin/delta (extfile) ~/.config/nvim (extdir) One line per tracked file, decoded from source/ magic names into the destination path you actually care about. Flag annotations show when something unusual is happening to the file — private means 0600, template means it's rendered through Tera before writing, extdir means it's a remote git clone, and so on. Files matching config/ignore patterns are excluded, same as apply and status . haven telemetry — manage telemetry from the CLI ¶ haven telemetry --enable # set [telemetry] enabled = true in haven.toml haven telemetry --disable # set [telemetry] enabled = false in haven.toml haven telemetry # show current status haven telemetry --note "starting fresh config — prior data is from testing" Previously, enabling telemetry required manually editing haven.toml . --enable and --disable handle it with one command (using toml_edit internally, so your existing comments and formatting are preserved). --note appends a free-form annotation to ~/.haven/telemetry.jsonl regardless of whether telemetry is enabled. Use it to mark test runs, onboarding sessions, or observed issues — the notes become context when analysing the telemetry log later. Shell completions ¶ # Fish — write once: haven completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/haven.fish # Zsh — add to ~/.zshrc: source <(haven completions zsh) # Bash — add to ~/.bashrc: source <(haven completions bash) All subcommands and flags are included, including the new security-scan and completions commands themselves. Jujutsu (jj) VCS backend ¶ # Set globally in haven.toml: [vcs] backend = "jj" # Or per-command: haven init gh:you/env --vcs jj # Or via env var: HAVEN_VCS=jj haven init When jj is configured, all new extdir_ clones and haven init operations use jj git clone --colocate instead of git clone . Repos get both .jj/ and .git/ , so both tools work in them. On first use without a config, haven detects whether jj is on your PATH and prompts once (save, use-once, or stay with git). The choice is persisted in haven.toml . haven vcs # show active backend and how it was resolved Other changes ¶ Getting started guide for chezmoi users — new docs/from-chezmoi.md walks existing chezmoi users through migration. Covers the automated import path, template syntax conversion, command equivalence table, Brewfile/module/ profile setup, AI skill import, new-machine bootstrap, and a gap table of chezmoi features not yet in haven. config/ignore warning on haven add — adding a file that matches an ignore pattern now shows a clear message instead of silently succeeding. Installer guide section — the installation documentation was expanded with step-by-step instructions. Upgrade notes ¶ No breaking changes. Existing repos work without modification. If you track ~/.ssh/** , ~/.kube/** , or similar paths, haven security-scan will flag them as expected. Add them to [security] allow in haven.toml to suppress the findings if you're intentionally tracking those files. What's next (planned for v0.4.0) ¶ haven security-scan --fix — automatically remove High-severity files after confirmation Pre-push hook support ( haven init --hooks ) --from yadm and --from mackup importers brew install haven distribution via Homebrew tap Periodic automatic update checks ( [update] check = "weekly" in haven.toml )

docs.text: Define your own variables in haven.toml and use them in any .tmpl file: [data] host = "my-laptop" kanata_path = "/usr/local/bin/kanata" # In any .tmpl file: export HOST={{ data.host }} {{ if data.host }}# host-specific config{% endif %} Variables are accessible as {{ data.<key> }} . Nested objects and non-string values are not supported — only flat string keys. haven data prints all variables currently in scope: haven data os = macos hostname = my-laptop username = alice home_dir = /Users/alice source_dir = /Users/alice/.local/share/haven data.host = my-laptop data.kanata_path = /usr/local/bin/kanata Chezmoi import: haven import --from chezmoi automatically migrates .chezmoidata.yaml and .chezmoidata.toml into [data] entries in haven.toml . The template converter also updates .tmpl files — chezmoi's {{ .host }} becomes {{ data.host }} and {{ if (index . "host") }} becomes {% if data.host %} .
docs.title: [data] custom template variables

docs.text: haven unmanaged # scan ~ for untracked dotfiles haven unmanaged --path ~/.config # scan a specific directory haven unmanaged --depth 5 # recurse deeper (default: 3) Walks your home directory and reports every file that isn't tracked by haven. At the home root, only dotfiles and dotdirs (names starting with . ) are examined — Documents/ , Downloads/ , Projects/ , etc. are always skipped. High-noise directories are skipped at every depth: Category Directories skipped VCS internals .git , .jj , .hg , .svn Build / caches node_modules , target , .cache , .cargo , .npm , .rustup , .pyenv , .rbenv , .nvm , .volta , .asdf , .mise , .gradle , .m2 macOS system Library , .Trash , .Spotlight-V100 , .DocumentRevisions-V100 , .fseventsd , .TemporaryItems App-managed .android , .minikube , .kube , .docker , .vscode-server , .cocoapods , .bundle haven state .haven Pipe the output to haven add to bulk-import discovered files: haven unmanaged | xargs -I{} haven add {}
docs.title: haven unmanaged — discover dotfiles you haven't added yet

docs.text: haven list ~/.zshrc ~/.gitconfig (template) ~/.ssh/config (private) ~/.local/bin/delta (extfile) ~/.config/nvim (extdir) One line per tracked file, decoded from source/ magic names into the destination path you actually care about. Flag annotations show when something unusual is happening to the file — private means 0600, template means it's rendered through Tera before writing, extdir means it's a remote git clone, and so on. Files matching config/ignore patterns are excluded, same as apply and status .
docs.title: haven list — see what you're tracking

docs.text: haven telemetry --enable # set [telemetry] enabled = true in haven.toml haven telemetry --disable # set [telemetry] enabled = false in haven.toml haven telemetry # show current status haven telemetry --note "starting fresh config — prior data is from testing" Previously, enabling telemetry required manually editing haven.toml . --enable and --disable handle it with one command (using toml_edit internally, so your existing comments and formatting are preserved). --note appends a free-form annotation to ~/.haven/telemetry.jsonl regardless of whether telemetry is enabled. Use it to mark test runs, onboarding sessions, or observed issues — the notes become context when analysing the telemetry log later.
docs.title: haven telemetry — manage telemetry from the CLI

docs.text: # Fish — write once: haven completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/haven.fish # Zsh — add to ~/.zshrc: source <(haven completions zsh) # Bash — add to ~/.bashrc: source <(haven completions bash) All subcommands and flags are included, including the new security-scan and completions commands themselves.
docs.title: Shell completions

docs.text: # Set globally in haven.toml: [vcs] backend = "jj" # Or per-command: haven init gh:you/env --vcs jj # Or via env var: HAVEN_VCS=jj haven init When jj is configured, all new extdir_ clones and haven init operations use jj git clone --colocate instead of git clone . Repos get both .jj/ and .git/ , so both tools work in them. On first use without a config, haven detects whether jj is on your PATH and prompts once (save, use-once, or stay with git). The choice is persisted in haven.toml . haven vcs # show active backend and how it was resolved
docs.title: Jujutsu (jj) VCS backend

docs.text: Getting started guide for chezmoi users — new docs/from-chezmoi.md walks existing chezmoi users through migration. Covers the automated import path, template syntax conversion, command equivalence table, Brewfile/module/ profile setup, AI skill import, new-machine bootstrap, and a gap table of chezmoi features not yet in haven. config/ignore warning on haven add — adding a file that matches an ignore pattern now shows a clear message instead of silently succeeding. Installer guide section — the installation documentation was expanded with step-by-step instructions.
docs.title: Other changes

docs.text: No breaking changes. Existing repos work without modification. If you track ~/.ssh/** , ~/.kube/** , or similar paths, haven security-scan will flag them as expected. Add them to [security] allow in haven.toml to suppress the findings if you're intentionally tracking those files.
docs.title: Upgrade notes

docs.text: haven security-scan --fix — automatically remove High-severity files after confirmation Pre-push hook support ( haven init --hooks ) --from yadm and --from mackup importers brew install haven distribution via Homebrew tap Periodic automatic update checks ( [update] check = "weekly" in haven.toml )
docs.title: What's next (planned for v0.4.0)

docs.text: haven v0.4.0 Release Notes ¶ Release date: 2026-03-23 Highlights ¶ The project is now called haven ¶ The tool has been renamed from dfiles to haven . The name change is the primary reason for the 0.4.0 version bump. Why? "dfiles" sounds like "defiles" when spoken aloud, and df conflicts with the standard Unix df (disk free) command. haven — a place of shelter, a home for your configuration — is a better fit for what the tool does. What changed: Before After dfiles (binary) haven dfiles.toml haven.toml ~/.dfiles/ ~/.haven/ DFILES_DIR HAVEN_DIR DFILES_VCS HAVEN_VCS DFILES_TELEMETRY HAVEN_TELEMETRY DFILES_PROFILE HAVEN_PROFILE GitHub johnstegeman/dfiles johnstegeman/haven The source encoding, repo layout, and file format are unchanged . Existing repos migrate by renaming dfiles.toml → haven.toml and ~/.dfiles/ → ~/.haven/ . New installer URL: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnstegeman/haven/main/install.sh | sh config/ignore is now a Tera template ¶ The ignore file at config/ignore is now rendered through the Tera engine before patterns are evaluated — exactly how chezmoi handles .chezmoiignore . This means you can write OS- or hostname-conditional ignore patterns: {% if os == "macos" %} .DS_Store {% endif %} {% if hostname == "work-laptop" %} .config/work-secrets/** {% endif %} All variables available in .tmpl files ( os , hostname , username , profile , data.* ) work in config/ignore too. If rendering fails, haven warns and falls back to ignoring nothing (safe degradation, not a crash). haven import --from chezmoi now converts Go template syntax in .chezmoiignore to Tera syntax rather than stripping template lines. haven init is now a safe no-op on an existing repo ¶ Running haven init in a directory that's already a haven repo used to error out. It now prints an informational message and exits 0. This makes it safe to call idempotently in bootstrap scripts. haven --version shows full build identity ¶ haven 0.4.0+abc1234 The version output now includes the short git commit hash, matching the version field in telemetry events. Useful when filing bug reports. Upgrade ¶ # Re-install from the new URL curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnstegeman/haven/main/install.sh | sh # Rename your config and state directory mv ~/haven.toml ~/.local/share/haven/haven.toml 2>/dev/null || true mv ~/.dfiles ~/.haven 2>/dev/null || true
docs.title: haven v0.4.0 Release Notes

docs.text: The tool has been renamed from dfiles to haven . The name change is the primary reason for the 0.4.0 version bump. Why? "dfiles" sounds like "defiles" when spoken aloud, and df conflicts with the standard Unix df (disk free) command. haven — a place of shelter, a home for your configuration — is a better fit for what the tool does. What changed: Before After dfiles (binary) haven dfiles.toml haven.toml ~/.dfiles/ ~/.haven/ DFILES_DIR HAVEN_DIR DFILES_VCS HAVEN_VCS DFILES_TELEMETRY HAVEN_TELEMETRY DFILES_PROFILE HAVEN_PROFILE GitHub johnstegeman/dfiles johnstegeman/haven The source encoding, repo layout, and file format are unchanged . Existing repos migrate by renaming dfiles.toml → haven.toml and ~/.dfiles/ → ~/.haven/ . New installer URL: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnstegeman/haven/main/install.sh | sh

docs.text: The ignore file at config/ignore is now rendered through the Tera engine before patterns are evaluated — exactly how chezmoi handles .chezmoiignore . This means you can write OS- or hostname-conditional ignore patterns: {% if os == "macos" %} .DS_Store {% endif %} {% if hostname == "work-laptop" %} .config/work-secrets/** {% endif %} All variables available in .tmpl files ( os , hostname , username , profile , data.* ) work in config/ignore too. If rendering fails, haven warns and falls back to ignoring nothing (safe degradation, not a crash). haven import --from chezmoi now converts Go template syntax in .chezmoiignore to Tera syntax rather than stripping template lines.
docs.title: config/ignore is now a Tera template

docs.text: Running haven init in a directory that's already a haven repo used to error out. It now prints an informational message and exits 0. This makes it safe to call idempotently in bootstrap scripts.
docs.title: haven init is now a safe no-op on an existing repo

docs.text: haven 0.4.0+abc1234 The version output now includes the short git commit hash, matching the version field in telemetry events. Useful when filing bug reports.
